achievements: Complete infrastructure migration from Digital Ocean to AWS
Full backend overhaul improving performance and security
project.description: Migrated Chanced.com from Digital Ocean to AWS and performed a complete overhaul of their PHP Laravel backend. The project modernized the hosting infrastructure and improved application performance and reliability.
project.title: Digital Ocean to AWS Migration & Backend Overhaul
project.technicalApproach: Migrated hosting from Digital Ocean to AWS. Overhauled the entire PHP Laravel backend for improved performance, security, and maintainability. Leveraged AWS services for hosting, storage, and compute.

matchedRequirements: Performance must be USA-based and from a US company

achievements: Secure, localized trading platform with CMS-driven content
Automated CI/CD pipeline with Azure Pipelines
Global performance optimization with Cloudflare CDN
project.description: Built a secure cryptocurrency trading platform frontend with dynamic, localized content. The platform features server-side rendering, CMS-driven content management, and CDN optimization for global performance.
project.title: Secure Crypto Trading Platform
project.technicalApproach: Developed the frontend using Next.js with Strapi CMS for dynamic content management. Set up CI/CD with Azure Pipelines for automated deployments. Applied Cloudflare optimizations for global performance and security.

matchedRequirements: Performance must be USA-based and from a US company

achievements: High-performance web application with server-side rendering
project.description: Created a performant front-end for an Amplify-hosted web application using Next.js. The platform features server-side rendering for optimal performance and is deployed on AWS Amplify for streamlined hosting and CI/CD.
project.title: Performant Web Application on AWS Amplify
project.technicalApproach: Built the application with Next.js and TypeScript, leveraging server-side rendering for performance. Deployed on AWS Amplify with integrated hosting and continuous deployment pipelines.

matchedRequirements: Performance must be USA-based and from a US company

achievements: Full multitenant HR platform supporting multiple organizations
Integrated video interviewing and resume parsing capabilities
Streamlined candidate workflows from application to hire
project.description: Built a multitenant human resources application that integrates video interviewing, resume parsing, and candidate workflows. The platform supports multiple organizations on a single deployment with tenant isolation, enabling end-to-end recruitment process automation.
project.title: Multitenant HR Platform with Video Interviewing
project.technicalApproach: Developed a React-based frontend with AWS serverless backend. Integrated video interviewing capabilities, AI-powered resume parsing, and candidate workflow management. Built with multi-tenancy support for organizational isolation.

matchedRequirements: Provide capability that integrates data from disparate data sources so that insights can be gained from a single query across multiple data sources

achievements: Automated translation of safety manuals from German to multiple languages
Eliminated manual translation and formatting workflows
Maintained document formatting integrity across languages
project.description: Built an automated transcription and translation system for safety manuals, converting German-language PDF articles into every required country-specific language. The platform ingests PDFs, extracts text, translates content, and regenerates formatted PDF output.
project.title: Multilingual Safety Manual Translation Platform
project.technicalApproach: Used Amazon Textract to extract text from PDF safety manuals. Integrated Amazon Translate for multilingual conversion. Built an automated pipeline to reconvert translated text back into formatted PDF documents.

recommendations: 34 critical gap(s) identified. Consider teaming arrangements or subcontractors with relevant experience.
Overall past performance coverage is below 70%. This may significantly impact win probability.
criticalGaps: Provide global origination and destination (OD) data, fare data, service class information, fair share/QSI data, and local and flow data by route and carrier

Provide global schedule data by airline including historical, current, and future schedule information with flight level granularity: flight number, arrival and departure times, aircraft type, number of seats on aircraft on a market-by-market basis
Provide current and historical fleet information per airline (fleet database) with capacity and cabin configuration, including seat capacity and cabin class by aircraft and flight
Provide advanced booking data by market and airline derived from airline industry sources such as airlines, global distribution systems (GDS), or screen scraping providers
Data should be available going back to at least 2005, and include attribute data for airports and air carriers
Provide OAG, Innovata or comparable schedule product updates, minimum of weekly
Provide regular updates as frequently as possible (monthly preferred) to the demand and fare file including updated traffic, fare, load factor, booking, and on-time performance data
Solution must support a minimum of 10 users with dedicated usernames and passwords

Provide a functionally robust user interface (UI) enabling analysts to create output by non-directional or directional, marketing carrier, operating carrier, or both, and aggregations by month, year, or quarter
Users should be able to make custom reports in addition to having access to pre-defined reports for easy and efficient access to information
Access to the tool, the ability to run it and query the hosted data should be on a 24/7 basis
Provide bulk data export capability (CSV/TXT formatted files) for ingestion into other tools
Include Customer Support Services with an online customer support website and/or telephone number
Solution must be web-based and accessible through a website without requiring software installation on DOT systems
Provide capability that integrates data from disparate data sources so that insights can be gained from a single query across multiple data sources
Provide detailed explanation of specific data sources and methodologies employed to collect, process, aggregate, and validate data (per Amendment 00007)

Provide detailed AI Integration Plans describing the role AI will play, how AI is integrated into back-end and front-end UI/UX, and mechanisms for system transparency and user verification (per Amendment 00007)

For AI-included offers, describe guardrails (content filters, anti-prompt injection defense, bias mitigation, grounding and fact-checking, operational/action constraints) and AI model management to prevent hallucinated or compromised analysis (per Amendment 00007)
For AI-included offers, describe methodology for testing AI tool prior to and during deployment (per Amendment 00007)
Detail exact mechanisms and protocols to ensure overall accuracy of data and any AI-generated outputs or business intelligence reports (per Amendment 00007)
Comprehensively describe the functionality and capability of the interface demonstrating how the UI supports custom reports, advanced booking evaluations, and market queries (per Amendment 00007)
Provide modular pricing separating costs of raw data provision from interface and AI tools (per Amendment 00007)

Provide three days of new user or refresher training for up to 10 users, or three days of advanced user training for up to three users, each year as needed, at DOT offices in Washington DC or virtually via Zoom or Microsoft Teams with 90-day advance notice
Provide a demonstration of the solution to DOT personnel prior to submitting a proposal; schedule demonstration by 12pm EST on or before March 2, 2026
Contractor staff shall have a minimum of five (5) years experience gathering, compiling and analyzing data and software, congruent certifications held, and minimum Bachelor's Degree
Identify Key Personnel who shall be reachable by phone twenty-four hours a day with backup personnel of equal or greater knowledge and experience

Protect privacy of individuals in accordance with the Privacy Act of 1974 (5 USC 552a) and meet requirements of NIST FIPS 200 Minimum Security Requirements for Federal Information and Information Systems
Follow requirements in DOT Information Technology and Information Assurance Policy 034 for Reporting Cyber Security Incidents and Sensitive PII Exposures
All onsite employees will sign Non-disclosure Agreement (Attachment 1)
Contractor must not provide covered telecommunications equipment or services per FAR 52.204-25 (Section 889 NDAA FY2019)
Contractor must be registered in SAM with current, accurate, and complete representations and certifications
Performance must be USA-based and from a US company
Prior to removing, replacing, or diverting any Key Personnel, Contractor shall notify in writing and receive consent from the Contracting Officer
Proposal valid for 90 days from date delivered to DOT
